untranslatable_reason: Signal requires monitoring of option-specific metrics (intrinsic value, parity value, moneyness ratio, implied volatility stress testing) which are not available in the standard price/technical indicator set. The rule depends on: (1) option pricing models to calculate parity and moneyness, (2) IV stress scenarios with specific basis point thresholds, and (3) days-to-expiration countdown logic. No allowed indicators can directly measure these derivatives-market variables.
translation_notes: This signal is a derivative/options-specific trading rule from Natenberg that cannot be translated into equity price-action indicators. The rule requires access to: option Greeks (delta), implied volatility levels and stress tests, intrinsic vs. extrinsic value decomposition, and parity calculations. Standard OHLCV and technical indicators cannot replicate this decision logic. A proper implementation would require an options analytics module with access to underlying price, strike, rate, dividend, DTE, and IV surface data.
